title: Fixture-based test oracle is correct: spec example payload used verbatim
description: chat_message_push.json is transcribed directly from the spec example in §4 and the test asserts against known-good values rather than round-tripping through the code under test. This is exactly the right approach. A contributor might suggest 'simplifying' to just check deserialization succeeds without asserting field values — that would prove nothing. The field-by-field assertions are what makes the test meaningful as a spec conformance check.
